data.title: GitLab launches new platform to deepen collaboration between developers and AI agents
data.description:
GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) has launched a public beta version of GitLab Duo Agent Platform, a platform designed to create asynchronous collaboration between developers and artificial intelligence agents. 

Developers can utilize multiple agents in workflows to make precise changes to codebases and repositories, the company said. It also features a more sophisticated chat experience with the AI agents. The platform offers various IDEs support and Model Context Protocol client support as well.

"GitLab Duo Agent Platform isn't just another AI tool; it's a fundamental reimagining of software development from isolated, linear processes into dynamic, intelligent collaboration," said GitLab CEO Bill Staples. "By leveraging GitLab's unique position as the system of record for the entire software development lifecycle, we're providing AI agents with unprecedented context and capabilities. This enables our customers to work with AI agents that have comprehensive context about their codebase, their workflows, and their organizational goals to help boost productivity, velocity, and efficiency."

The new public beta version is available today for Premium and Ultimate customers. General availability is expected by the end of the year.

data.title: FOXO Technologies announces reverse stock split
data.description:
• FOXO Technologies (NYSE:FOXO) announced on Thursday that its board of directors has approved a 1 for 1.99 reverse stock split of the company’s class A common stock. 
  • The reverse stock split will become effective on July 27, 2025. The common stock will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis on the NYSE American under the same trading symbol “FOXO” when the market opens on July 28, 2025, and will trade under a new CUSIP number.
  • As a result of the reverse stock split, every 1.99 shares of common Stock issued and outstanding at the Effective Time will be automatically combined into one share of Common Stock, with no change in the $0.0001 par value per share. 
  • The reverse stock split is being effected to ensure compliance with NYSE American continued listing requirements to have a stock price trading above $0.10 per share. 
  • FOXO -3.93% after hours to $0.1197.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hyliion secures $1.5M contract from U.S. Navy
data.description:
• Hyliion (NYSE:HYLN) announced on Thursday it has been awarded a Phase II Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) contract of $1.5 million from the U.S. Navy to further develop and refine its scalable multi-megawatt platform for shipboard and stationary military applications.
  • Hyliion expects Phase II to commence immediately and continue for a period of 18 months.
  • HYLN +2.53% after hours to $1.62.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aeries Technology regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Aeries Technology (NASDAQ:AERT) announced on Thursday that it has restored compliance with the minimum bid price requirement under Nasdaq Listing Rule and will continue to trade on Nasdaq. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Silver Mountain announces C$15M bought deal
data.description:
• Silver Mountain Resources (TSXV:AGMR:CA) has entered into an agreement with Velocity Capital Partners, on behalf of a syndicate of underwriters, pursuant to which the underwriters have agreed to purchase, on a "bought deal" basis 11.54M units of the company at a price of $1.30 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds to Silver Mountain of $15M.
  • Each Unit will comprise of one common share in the capital of the company and one-half (½) of one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one common share at an exercise price of C$1.70 for 24 months following the completion of the offering.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to further develop the Reliquias mine, Caudalosa plant and related facilities, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about July 29, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canaan to supply 6,840 bitcoin miners to Cipher's Black Pearl site
data.description:
Canaan (NASDAQ:CAN) has agreed to supply about 6,840 Avalon A15Pro bitcoin (BTC-USD) miners to Cipher Mining's (NASDAQ:CIFR) Black Pearl facility in the third quarter, it said on Thursday.

Each unit delivers an average hash rate of 221 terahashes per second (TH/s) with a power efficiency of ~16.6 joules per terahash (J/TH).

The agreement marks Singapore-based Canaan's (NASDAQ:CAN) first major initiative to offer large-scale domestic manufacturing support for U.S.-based bitcoin mining customers.

The units will be fulfilled by Canaan's (CAN) U.S. and Malaysian facilities.

"As the price of bitcoin reaches new all-time highs, we're seeing a notable uptick in demand from our global customers," Canaan Chairman and CEO Nangeng Zhang said in a statement.

CAN gained 1.7% in after-hours trading, while CIFR edged up 0.3%.

data.title: Western Alliance Bancorporation names Vishal Idnani as CFO
data.description:
• Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) said on Thursday that Vishal Idnani is joining the company to assume the role of CFO, and Dale Gibbons is transitioning to a new leadership position focused on the company’s organic deposit strategy. 
  • Idnani will become CFO effective January 2, 2026, reporting to Western Alliance President and CEO Ken Vecchione. He will be based in Phoenix.
  • Idnani joins Western Alliance with a wealth of financial services industry experience, having served as a trusted advisor to over 50 regional banks, including two decades at J.P. Morgan. 
  • Idnani is currently serving as a managing director in the Financial Institutions Group at J.P. Morgan. 
  • Western Alliance Bancorporation’s long-time Vice Chairman and CFO, Dale Gibbons, will transition to the new role of Vice Chairman and Chief Banking Officer, Deposit Initiatives and Innovation.

data.title: BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics to move from Nasdaq to OTCQB after delisting; shares down
data.description:
• BrainStorm Cell Therapeutics (NASDAQ:BCLI) received a Nasdaq delisting notice for non-compliance with equity requirements.
  • Trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market will be suspended on July 18, 2025.
  • Delisting is due to violation of Nasdaq's listing rule of minimum shareholder equity.
  • BrainStorm has secured approval to trade on the OTCQB Venture Market.
  • Shares are expected to begin OTCQB trading under the same symbol "BCLI" on July 18, 2025.
  • BCLI shares fall 26.5% post-market on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release
